title: Draft: DSP-7746 Add redis-monitoring helm chart
description:
## Description
_Add a description so that technical users can better understand what this MR does._

## MR Checklist

- [ ] Is there a Jira ticket in the title of this MR?
- [ ] Are the summary notes in the Jira ticket filled in with a sentence describing the change to non-technical users?
- [ ] Has the `breaking` label been applied, if this is a breaking change?
- [ ] Has the `new-feature` label been added, if this change adds a new feature?

title: DSP-8022 Add priority-class helm chart
description:
## Description
- Adds priority classes that will be used to decide eviction during instance refresh. 
- This will be used by redis but can be extended to anything else

## MR Checklist

- [x] Is there a Jira ticket in the title of this MR?
- [x] Are the summary notes in the Jira ticket filled in with a sentence describing the change to non-technical users?
- [ ] Has the `breaking` label been applied, if this is a breaking change?
- [ ] Has the `new-feature` label been added, if this change adds a new feature?

title: DSP-8021 DSP-8022 Add sentinel and antiAffinity for redis
description:
## Description
- Adds anti-affinity to redis pods so that they can get scheduled on different nodes
- Adds priority classes so that during node refreshes the replicas can evict first, then the master
- Adds PDBs
- Adds terminationGracePeriod, lifecyclehooks and startUpProbes


## MR Checklist

- [x] Is there a Jira ticket in the title of this MR?
- [x] Are the summary notes in the Jira ticket filled in with a sentence describing the change to non-technical users?
- [ ] Has the `breaking` label been applied, if this is a breaking change?
- [ ] Has the `new-feature` label been added, if this change adds a new feature?

title: Draft: [Part3] ClamAV version is updated to 1.4.3 Resolve DSP-7172
description:
Although the ticket specifies updating the ClamAV version to 1.0.9 (as per the ClamAV documentation in the console), I found that the most recent version is 1.4.3. This is part 3 of the MRs for the luna-bakery repository, and this MR cannot be merged at this time. It can only be merged once we start baking AL2023 AMIs in the production environments.

sh-5.2$ sudo clamscan --version
ClamAV 1.4.3/27787/Thu Oct  9 09:31:07 2025
sh-5.2$ sudo freshclam --version
ClamAV 1.4.3/27787/Thu Oct  9 09:31:07 2025

title: Draft: [6789] PART 2 - luna-bakery - AL2023 Migration
description:
This MR updates the luna-bakery customization scripts executed during the AMI baking process to support AL2023 AMIs. 

This MR should remain unmerged until we are prepared to start baking AL2023 AMIs for production environments and commence the production migration to AL2023.

title: Draft: [DSP-6573] PART 1 AMI Transition from AL2 to AL2023
description:
There will be numerous merge requests (MRs) for this epic, primarily affecting luna-bakery, ng-infrastructure, terraform-aws-eks module, eks-worker, and eks-cluster module for 1.0 infrastructure. Additional MRs may be required once we complete the IMDS transition from IMDSv1 to IMDSv2 calls. I can only merge all these MRs when every component is ready and the IMDS migration is complete. I've begun raising MRs early to gather reviews and address questions about the changes being implemented for this epic.

This Part 1 of the MR addresses naming convention issues encountered with AL2023 AMIs. The base image name used for AMI filtering has changed from 'eks-node-1.32' to 'eks-node-al2023-x86_64-standard-1.32' in AL2023, which significantly altered the AMI tags and names generated for baked AL2023 AMIs. This MR primarily contains changes to maintain naming conventions consistent with those used for AL2 AMIs. Additionally, since SELinux is set to permissive mode by default in AL2023, I've removed the SELinux script and its associated configuration from the template.

Pipeline airflow deploy using the DSP-8091 orchestrator branch with metrics in prometheus:

https://gitlab.com/abacusinsights/dispatch-dev/ng-manifest-cd/-/jobs/14204552708

![image](/uploads/4b80b5f6f88b5bd57163f289c018ed84/image.png){width=900 height=439}

title: [DSP-8091] [1/3] Enable native Seiji runs to export OTEL metrics to tenant cluster collector
description:
`nextgen.otel.provision`
- Adds a new `otel_external_collector_enabled` config if we ever need to disable the external collector (defaults to `false`).
- Adds ACM cert in the public zone and security group that only allows port 443 from our `admin_cidrs` to connect to the external collector ALB through the annotation `alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/security-groups`.


`nextgen.otel.deploy`

- Adds a new external collector service with an ALB ingress that allows traffic from outside the kubernetes cluster (but must be within `admin_cidrs`) to push metrics to otel using HTTP. 

The original ask was to allow applications to push to the collector using gRPC but I ran into lots of networking issues that I was not able to solve in the communication between the ALB and the gRPC ingress. Port forwarding the external service on port 4317 (gRPC default port) works, but when trying to send metrics through the FQDN and ingress, connections were getting dropped. The workaround was possibly creating a target group specifically with the gRPC backend protocol (which the AWS load balancer controller did not support) and assigning it to the load balancer, but this step would have to happen _after_ the ALB gets created which makes deployment order tricky. 

<details>
<summary>Test posting a metric to the external collector and verifying in prometheus</summary>

```
echo "== OTLP/HTTP JSON POST https://${HOST}/v1/metrics =="
NOW_NS="$(python3 -c 'import time; print(int(time.time()*1e9))')"
HTTP_CODE="$(
  curl -sS ${CURL_RESOLVE_OPTS+"${CURL_RESOLVE_OPTS[@]}"} ${CURL_TLS_OPTS+"${CURL_TLS_OPTS[@]}"} -o /tmp/otel_ext_otlp_resp.txt -w '%{http_code}' \
    --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 30 \
    -X POST "https://${HOST}/v1/metrics" \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d @- <<EOF
{
  "resourceMetrics": [{
    "resource": {
      "attributes": [{ "key": "service.name", "value": { "stringValue": "manual-otlp-http-test" } }]
    },
    "scopeMetrics": [{
      "metrics": [{
        "name": "manual.test.counter",
        "sum": {
          "aggregationTemporality": "AGGREGATION_TEMPORALITY_CUMULATIVE",
          "isMonotonic": true,
          "dataPoints": [{
            "asDouble": 1,
            "timeUnixNano": "${NOW_NS}"
          }]
        }
      }]
    }]
  }]
}
EOF
)"
echo "HTTP status: ${HTTP_CODE}"
if [[ "${HTTP_CODE}" != "200" ]]; then
  echo "Response body:" >&2
  cat /tmp/otel_ext_otlp_resp.txt >&2 || true
  exit 1
fi
```

```
➜ HOST=otel-collector.alexwest.dispatch.abacusinsights.ai ./scripts/healthcheck/test_external_collector_otlp.sh
== TLS / DNS (curl SNI): https://otel-collector.alexwest.dispatch.abacusinsights.ai/ ==
http_code:404 ssl_verify:0 connect_s:0.105575 tls_s:0.167063
== OTLP/HTTP JSON POST https://otel-collector.alexwest.dispatch.abacusinsights.ai/v1/metrics ==
HTTP status: 200
OK: OTLP HTTP export accepted (200).
```

title: Draft: [CMD-] NextGen 26.3.45 Release
description:
## Description
This change releases onyx-helmsman @ 26.3.4 to customers, including:

| Ticket | Summary Notes | Breaking? |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [DSP-7670](https://abacusinsights.atlassian.net/browse/DSP-7670) | fluentbit for OnyxInsights reports PutObject API responded with error='AccessDenied'  | No |
| [DSP-7732](https://abacusinsights.atlassian.net/browse/DSP-7732) | Create p2p validator helm chart | No |
| [DSP-7835](https://abacusinsights.atlassian.net/browse/DSP-7835) | [P2P] Implement BulkMemberMatch Export functionality and infrastructure | No |
| [DSP-7866](https://abacusinsights.atlassian.net/browse/DSP-7866) | nextgen.onyx.deploy oauth2-proxy/templates/secret.yaml failure | No |
| [DSP-7883](https://abacusinsights.atlassian.net/browse/DSP-7883) | Update org slug for import to show up in onyxpayer-ui | No |
| [DSP-7896](https://abacusinsights.atlassian.net/browse/DSP-7896) | Integrate FITE V2.5.1 for ONYX-403 | No |
| [DSP-7943](https://abacusinsights.atlassian.net/browse/DSP-7943) | Fix a typo in onyxpayer-user-credentials helmchart | No |
| [DSP-7952](https://abacusinsights.atlassian.net/browse/DSP-7952) | Integrate P2P v2.2.2 for ONYX-417/ONXY-478/ONYX-490 | No |
| [DSP-7956](https://abacusinsights.atlassian.net/browse/DSP-7956) | nextgen.onyx.deploy fails in onyxpayer-ui failure due to unkonwn field error | No |
| [XPORT-2102](https://abacusinsights.atlassian.net/browse/XPORT-2102) | EPA ingress doesn't redirect HTTP traffic to HTTPS | No |
| [XPORT-2109](https://abacusinsights.atlassian.net/browse/XPORT-2109) | Integrate onyx  ePA Deliverable-5 - Vulnerability fixes ONYX-401 ONYX-400 | No |

## MR Checklist

- [ ] Is there a Jira ticket in the title of this MR?
- [ ] Are the summary notes in the Jira ticket filled in with a sentence describing the change to non-technical users?
- [ ] Has the `breaking` label been applied, if this is a breaking change?
- [ ] Has the `new-feature` label been added, if this change adds a new feature?

title: Draft: Add random facts to the mix
Draft: [DSP-6123] Automate airbyte ssh key generation
description:
## Description
Following https://gitlab.com/abacusinsights/abacus-v2/next-gen-platform/ng-infrastructure/-/merge_requests/1075, we now have the ability to generate SSH key secrets as part of deployment of the Airbyte component when needed. In the case of these three clients (Cambia, PHP, and Medstar) this secret has already been generated manually so we'll need to import those resources into Terraform before merging this MR (thus the `Draft` mode.

## MR Checklist

- [x] Is there a Jira ticket in the title of this MR?
- [ ] Are the summary notes in the Jira ticket filled in with a sentence describing the change to non-technical users?
- [ ] Has the `breaking` label been applied, if this is a breaking change?
- [ ] Has the `new-feature` label been added, if this change adds a new feature?

Related to DSP-6123
